data.title: Has Google’s AI watermarking system been reverse-engineered?

content.basic: A software developer claims to have reverse-engineered Google DeepMind's SynthID system, showing how AI watermarks can be stripped from generated images or manually inserted into other works. A claim that, according to Google, isn't true. The developer, going by the username Aloshdenny, has open-sourced their work on GitHub and documented his process, claiming all it required was 200 Gemini-generated images, signal processing, and "way too much free time." A little weed also seemed to help. "No neural networks. No proprietary access," Aloshdenny said on Medium. "Turns out if you're unemployed

data.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro

content.basic: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.
